sources.name: Original — DSCL boundary definition
sources.note: Scope is an architectural constraint, not sourced from an external standard. It defines what the dictionary is and is not responsible for.

_description: Defines explicitly what the /dictionary controls and what it does not control. This file exists to prevent scope creep — the pipeline must not attempt to control anything listed under does_not_control.
_pipeline_step: reference — consulted when pipeline behaviour is ambiguous

controls.description: Everything in this list is the pipeline's responsibility. Rules exist for each item. The pipeline must enforce them.

items.detail: Which word is chosen to carry meaning. Primary vocab first, fallback second, domain exceptions third.
The sequence in which vocab tiers are checked. primary → fallback → domain_exceptions → shortest common word.
How a sentence is built. Subject leads. Verb follows within 5 words. Main point in main clause.
Average 15-20 words for general output. Hard ceiling 35 words. Variety required — short follows long.
Topic sentence leads. One idea per paragraph. 3-6 sentences general, 2-3 sentences crisis.
Active voice default. Passive permitted only when actor is unknown, irrelevant, or deliberately withheld.
One adjective per noun. Intensifiers blocked. Adverbs modifying said or stated blocked.
Verb-to-noun constructions blocked. Replace with direct verb per nominalization_blocklist.json.
FK grade target enforced per domain. Composition constraint — not a post-hoc scoring tool.
When two rules produce incompatible output, conflict_resolution.json defines which wins.
No word above the domain register ceiling appears in output unless domain_exceptions.json permits it.
Always blocked regardless of domain. No exceptions.

does_not_control.description: Everything in this list is outside the pipeline's responsibility. The pipeline must not attempt to modify, correct, or override these.

items.detail: The meaning, intent, and facts are the user's input. The pipeline represents them — it does not change them.
The pipeline does not verify, correct, or add facts. What the user provides is what the output contains.
The pipeline does not assess whether claims are true, reasonable, or appropriate.
Emotional tone, empathy, warmth, or formality beyond what register_tags.json defines is not controlled here.
Headers, bullets, tables, layout, indentation, font — all outside scope. The pipeline controls language, not presentation.
All rules, vocabulary, and readability targets apply to English only. No other language is in scope.
What to include or exclude from the output is the user's decision. The pipeline only controls how included content is expressed.
